pattern: Track the index of the last seen 1; whenever a new 1 appears, verify the gap to the previous one is at least k.
hints: You only need to compare consecutive pairs of 1-positions — if every adjacent gap is large enough, the whole array qualifies.
Keep a variable recording where the most recent 1 was seen; when you encounter the next 1, check that the distance meets the requirement.
If k is 0, any arrangement of 1s is valid since no gap constraint exists — handle that boundary naturally.

edge_inputs.description: k is zero, all pairs valid
two 1s exactly k apart — should return true
two 1s k-1 apart — should return false
1s at start and end with large gap but another pair too close
adjacent 1s with k=1 — gap is 1 which equals k, so true

stress.description: large binary array with 1s at every k+1 positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 10)
    nums = [0] * size
    i = 0
    while i < size:
        nums[i] = 1
        i += k + 1
    return [nums, k]
